# @zakkster/lite-headless / tree

Headless tree-view per the WAI-ARIA APG tree pattern.
Hierarchical list with expand/collapse, single or multiple selection,
and full keyboard navigation.

## API

```js
createTree({
    selectionMode:       "single" | "multiple",   // default "single"
    defaultSelected,                              // string | string[] | null
    defaultExpanded,                              // array of keys initially open
    typeahead:           true,
    loop:                true,
    onSelectionChange:   (selected, reason) => {},
    onExpandedChange:    (expanded, reason) => {},
})

  attachRoot(el)                                   // role="tree" container
  attachNode(el, key, { disabled?, hasChildren? }) // each treeitem
  attachLabel(el, key)                             // optional: dedicated label
                                                   //   element for typeahead text

  // queries
  selected()                                       // string | string[] | null
  expanded()                                       // string[]
  isSelected(key) / isExpanded(key)
  isVisible(key)                                   // false if any ancestor closed
  hasChildren(key)
  focusKey(key)

  // mutations
  setSelected(v, reason?) | setExpanded(arr, reason?)
  select(key, reason?) | deselect(key, reason?) | toggleSelected(key, reason?)
  expand(key, reason?) | collapse(key, reason?) | toggleExpanded(key, reason?)
  expandAll() | collapseAll()
  setDisabled(key, flag)

  destroy() / destroyed
```

## Topology discovery

Parent is inferred from DOM nesting. `attachNode` walks each element's
ancestor chain looking for the nearest already-attached tree-node and
makes that the parent. Attach order matters: parents must be attached
before their children for the inference to find them. The element
wrapper guarantees DOM order via `createRoleObserver`.

For lazy-loaded subtrees: attach the parent first, then attach the
children later when their DOM is ready. The visible-list cache
invalidates on every attach so the next keyboard nav sees them.

## Keyboard (per APG tree pattern)

- `ArrowDown` / `ArrowUp`  ‚Äî next / previous visible item (wraps if `loop`)
- `ArrowRight` on collapsed parent  ‚Äî expand (focus stays)
- `ArrowRight` on expanded parent   ‚Äî move to first child
- `ArrowRight` on leaf              ‚Äî no-op
- `ArrowLeft` on expanded parent    ‚Äî collapse (focus stays)
- `ArrowLeft` on collapsed / leaf   ‚Äî move to parent
- `Home` / `End`            ‚Äî first / last visible
- `Enter`                   ‚Äî toggle expand (parent) or select (leaf)
- `Space`                   ‚Äî select (toggles in multiselect)
- `*`                       ‚Äî expand all sibling parents at the focused level
- typeahead                 ‚Äî single character; cycles through matches
- disabled items skipped during arrow nav and typeahead

## ARIA

Root:      `role="tree"`, `aria-multiselectable` when multiple-mode
Treeitem:  `role="treeitem"`, `aria-level`, `aria-setsize`,
           `aria-posinset`, `aria-selected`,
           `aria-expanded` (PARENTS ONLY -- leaves omit the attribute)
Group:     `role="group"` (when consumer marks the children container;
           optional)

Data attributes (consumer styling hooks):
- `data-tree-key`  ‚Äî the key for this node
- `data-open`     ‚Äî present when branch is expanded
- `data-leaf`     ‚Äî present when node has no children
- `data-selected`  ‚Äî "true" | "false"
- `data-disabled`  ‚Äî present when disabled

## Custom-element wrapper

```html
<lite-tree selection-mode="single" expanded="src,docs" selected="readme.md">
    <ul>
        <li data-tree-node="src">
            src
            <ul>
                <li data-tree-node="src/index.js">index.js</li>
                <li data-tree-node="src/util.js" data-disabled>util.js</li>
            </ul>
        </li>
        <li data-tree-node="docs">docs</li>
        <li data-tree-node="readme.md">readme.md</li>
    </ul>
</lite-tree>
```

Reactive attributes:
- `selected` ‚Äî string (single) or comma-separated keys (multi)
- `expanded` ‚Äî comma-separated keys

Read-once attributes:
- `selection-mode` ‚Äî "single" | "multiple"
- `no-typeahead`   ‚Äî presence disables typeahead
- `no-loop`        ‚Äî presence disables arrow wrap

Dispatches:
- `selectionchange` ‚Äî `detail: { selected, reason }`
- `expandedchange`  ‚Äî `detail: { expanded, reason }`

Mirrors active state back to attributes; the v0.7.9 cascade guard
(`if (reason !== "attribute")`) is applied so external setAttribute
flows don't bounce.

## Animation contract

The primitive writes `data-X="open|closed|leaf"`. Consumer owns
the CSS. Group containers can be animated via the same patterns as
accordion (grid-template-rows or interpolate-size):

```css
[:not([data-open])] + [role="group"] { display: none; }    /* no animation */

/* or animated: */
[role="group"] {
    display: grid; grid-template-rows: 1fr;
    transition: grid-template-rows 180ms ease;
}
[:not([data-open])] + [role="group"] { grid-template-rows: 0fr; }
[role="group"] > * { overflow: hidden; }
```

## CSS contract

This primitive's painted attributes follow the canonical CSS contract
in `docs/CSS_CONTRACT.md`. See that doc for the cross-primitive
taxonomy (booleans like `data-open` / `data-disabled` / `data-loading`,
enums like `data-status` / `data-side`, ARIA pairings, host accessor
naming, and event naming).
